notes: Christian community in Alexandria; home of the Catechetical School; major center of Greek-speaking Christianity from the late first century onward.
Early Christian community in Antioch associated with Barnabas and Paul.

Followers of Cerinthus active in Asia Minor c. 90-130; distinguished the earthly Jesus from the heavenly Christ who descended at baptism and departed before the crucifixion; opposed by Polycarp and the Johannine tradition in Ephesus per Irenaeus.
Christian community in Colossae addressed in Pauline correspondence.
Christian community in Corinth addressed in Pauline correspondence.
Movement gathered around Jesus during his ministry and carried forward in the apostolic era.
Early Christian heretical group denying Christ's physical incarnation and real bodily suffering; combated by Ignatius and Polycarp.

Jewish-Christian sect insisting on full Torah observance and rejecting the divine pre-existence of Christ; active in Syria-Palestine and Transjordan; refuted by Irenaeus (AH 1.26), Origen, and Epiphanius; name means the poor.
Christian community in Ephesus attested in Acts and Revelation.
Second Temple Jewish sect described by Josephus as a distinct school among the Jews.
Movement gathered around John the Baptist.
Political faction aligned with the Herodian ruling house and appearing in Gospel opposition scenes.
Jerusalem community central to the earliest apostolic period.
Christian community in Laodicea attested in Colossians and Revelation.
Movement founded by Marcion of Sinope c. 144 AD; rejected the OT God and canon; active in Rome; refuted by Irenaeus, Tertullian, and others.

Prophetic movement originating in Phrygia c. 170 under Montanus, Priscilla, and Maximilla; emphasized ongoing prophecy, strict asceticism, and rigorist discipline; spread across Asia, Rome, and North Africa; Tertullian later joined their cause.
Early Christian sect or faction named in Revelation and opposed in the messages to Ephesus and Pergamum.
Christian community in Pergamum attested in Revelation.
Jewish movement frequently appearing in the Gospel narratives.
Christian community in Philadelphia attested in Revelation.
Christian community in Philippi associated with Lydia and Pauline mission.
Sectarian Yahad community associated with Qumran literature.
Christian community in Rome addressed by Paul and associated with house-church gatherings.
Imperial polity framing much of the New Testament historical setting.
Priestly / elite group associated with Jerusalem leadership.
Distinct Israelite religious community centered on Samaria and Mount Gerizim.
Christian community in Sardis attested in Revelation.
Christian community in Smyrna attested in Revelation.
Christian community in Thessalonica attested in Acts and the Thessalonian letters.
Christian community in Thyatira attested in Revelation.
Gnostic school founded by Valentinus c. 140 AD; active in Rome and Alexandria; refuted extensively by Irenaeus in Against Heresies.
Late Second Temple revolutionary faction prominent in Jerusalem during the Jewish war according to Josephus.
